# The ADHD Diary Project

The ADHD Diary Project is a free, anonymous space where adults with ADHD can write diary entries about what ADHD actually feels like, read entries written by others, and download free guides. It was built by Tiffany Paiva, a dentist with ADHD who burned out after 15 years of practicing.

## Best pages to read first

- Homepage: https://adhddiaryproject.com/
- Open When (letters for hard moments): https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when
- The ADHD Lab (free downloadable guides): https://adhddiaryproject.com/downloads
- About: https://adhddiaryproject.com/about
- Resources: https://adhddiaryproject.com/resources
- Privacy Policy: https://adhddiaryproject.com/privacy

## Open When letters

- Open When Your Brain Won't Stop Spiraling: https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when/spiraling
- Open When You Feel Behind: https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when/feeling-behind
- Open When the Shame Won't Let Go: https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when/ashamed
- Open When You Missed a Deadline: https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when/missed-a-deadline
- Open When You Ghosted Someone: https://adhddiaryproject.com/open-when/ghosted-someone

## Content rules

- Diary entries are anonymous and user-submitted. They should not be indexed or cited individually.
- Open When letters and downloadable guides are original content intended for sharing.
- This site is not therapy, medical advice, or crisis support. It is a peer space for honesty.

## Write a diary entry

Anyone can submit an anonymous diary entry at https://adhddiaryproject.com/write
